title: Love Is A Many Strangled Thing

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene with Homer presumably making an error or experiencing mild misfortune
jokes.punchline: His iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase. Pure character integration but not original by this point in the series

jokes.text: Mr. Burns speaking French while checking hot air balloon checklist: 'Feu allumé. Oui. Ballon gonflé. Oui. Now enlevez le ballast!'
jokes.setup: Mr. Burns performing a routine balloon safety check
jokes.punchline: He randomly speaks French for no reason while doing a mundane task
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns pretentiousness - the random French fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Burns dramatically exclaiming 'Gadzooks! The one hazard this balloon cannot negotiate... a gentle breeze!'
jokes.setup: Burns in apparent danger in hot air balloon
jokes.punchline: The 'deadly threat' is just a light breeze, using archaic exclamation 'Gadzooks'
jokes.explanation: Great character voice with archaic language, plus the absurd anticlimax of 'gentle breeze'

jokes.text: Burns crying 'Help! Curtail my ascent! Curtail it, I say!'
jokes.setup: Burns needs rescue from balloon
jokes.punchline: Uses overly formal 'curtail my ascent' instead of 'help me down'
jokes.explanation: More Burns vocabulary comedy - formal language in a panic situation

jokes.text: Hey, where'd it go? Huh! I wonder if that's the same elk.
jokes.setup: Character mentions bagging an elk while hunting, then it disappears
jokes.punchline: Casually wonders if a completely different elk is the same one, suggesting confusion about basic cause and effect
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist humor about dim-witted character logic

jokes.text: Homer saying 'A balloon saved one of my arteries. It doesn't seem right to kill his cousin.'
jokes.setup: Homer considering shooting Burns' balloon
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre logic treating medical and toy balloons as family members
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - connecting unrelated balloons through medical procedure and family relationships

jokes.text: Moe going through elaborate shooting ritual including kissing and 'Mmm' sound before shooting
jokes.setup: Moe preparing to shoot Burns' balloon
jokes.punchline: His overly elaborate pre-shooting ritual with kiss and satisfied grunt
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's quirks, though not especially memorable

jokes.text: Burns crying 'Help! I'm wafting on a zephyr!'
jokes.setup: Burns still in distress from balloon
jokes.punchline: Uses poetic 'wafting on a zephyr' instead of 'blown by wind'
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Burns vocabulary moment - 'wafting on a zephyr' is beautifully pretentious

jokes.text: Insulting Homer's shooting with 'Nice shooting, Annie Oakley! If you were a biathlete, I'd say stick to skiing.'
jokes.setup: Homer missed his shot at the balloon
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult comparing him to famous sharpshooter then winter Olympic sport
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed insult with good escalation from Annie Oakley to biathlon

jokes.text: Burns offering 'All your money and all your stuff' / 'Lower and likelier' exchange
jokes.setup: Burns offers Homer any reward for saving him
jokes.punchline: Burns immediately shoots down Homer's greedy request with dry understatement
jokes.explanation: Great character interaction - Burns' deadpan response perfectly deflates Homer's greed

jokes.text: Ticket request degradation: 'Super Bowl tickets? Playoff tickets? Regular season, bobblehead day? Nothing-head day!'
jokes.setup: Homer trying to negotiate for tickets
jokes.punchline: Burns' escalating stinginess ending with made-up 'nothing-head day'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation structure with great payoff in 'nothing-head day' - Burns' cheapness taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Former player saying 'I wouldn't have to do this if I hadn't bought a Ferrari a day for 22 seasons.'
jokes.setup: Former NFL star working at day spa
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and excessive spending habit explanation
jokes.explanation: Good satire of athlete financial irresponsibility with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: 'I once hosted Saturday Night Live. Once again, ladies and gentlemen, Matchbox 20.'
jokes.setup: Player mentioning his TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to hosting SNL and introducing dated musical guest
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference joke but feels somewhat dated and not strongly integrated

jokes.text: Arts district 'with football-hating intellectuals! We'll stay for one inning, then we can go.'
jokes.setup: Stadium having an arts district for intellectuals who hate football
jokes.punchline: They use baseball terminology ('inning') while at a football game
jokes.explanation: Clever layered joke - intellectuals at football game using wrong sport's terminology

jokes.text: Stadium announcement: 'I hope you kids are enjoying yourself today because you and your children will be paying for this place long after the team moves to another city.'
jokes.setup: Public address at fancy new stadium
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest about public financing of sports stadiums
jokes.explanation: Excellent social satire - darkly funny truth about stadium financing delivered with perfect timing

jokes.text: Bart saying 'This song's a little bossy for me' about 'Everybody Dance Now'
jokes.setup: Fan cam playing dance music
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal interpretation of the song's commanding nature
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor - treating the song literally as being too commanding

jokes.text: Homer's logic: 'Marge, he's laughing. That means he's happy. Has the Joker taught you nothing?'
jokes.setup: Marge telling Homer to stop tickling
jokes.punchline: Homer uses Batman villain as parenting guidance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - using a psychotic villain as a reference for understanding emotions

jokes.text: Mr. Burns observing: 'The child has wetted his trousers!'
jokes.setup: Bart has peed his pants from being tickled
jokes.punchline: Burns' formal Victorian announcement of the incident
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns formal language applied to embarrassing situation - 'wetted his trousers' is perfectly pompous

jokes.text: Homer doing the wave alone: 'No one else is doing it. I'm trying to start it. Whoo!'
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out Homer is doing the wave alone
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious determination to start a wave by himself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Homer's social obliviousness on full display

jokes.text: News report: 'The stain was picked up by Russian spy satellites, and President Dmitry Medvedev has taken the pants-wetting as a sign of American weakness. A Russian flotilla has just entered New York harb...'
jokes.setup: News report about Bart's pants-wetting incident
jokes.punchline: Escalates to international incident and Russian military response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd escalation - child's embarrassing moment becomes geopolitical crisis. Classic Simpsons absurdism.

jokes.text: Homer: 'Well, it was your idea to give him self-esteem in the first place.'
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer destroyed Bart's self-esteem
jokes.punchline: Homer blames Marge for the concept of self-esteem itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer deflection - treating self-esteem as an optional parenting add-on rather than basic need

jokes.text: Marge saying 'Now I have to work overtime just to counteract the self-confidence...' then kids mocking: 'Hey Bart, I hear the forecast is for showers... in your pants! I'm on a European vacation, but you're a-peein' everywhere! Oui, oui!'
jokes.setup: Marge trying to rebuild Bart's confidence
jokes.punchline: Kids making elaborate puns about his pants-wetting incident
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with 'you're a-peein' and French 'oui oui' - multilingual pun structure

jokes.text: Marge finding fathering class in catalog after Homer says 'I'd like to see you thumb through an extension school catalog and find one.' 'Here's one right here.'
jokes.setup: Homer challenging Marge to find a fathering class
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately finds one, deflating his excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/deflation - Homer's overconfident challenge immediately backfires

jokes.text: The other negligent dads will make fun of me. They're so cliquey.
jokes.setup: Homer doesn't want to go to fathering class
jokes.punchline: Worries about social dynamics among specifically negligent fathers as a group
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Homer's social anxieties even extend to groups of bad fathers

jokes.text: Homer lamenting: 'Oh, I miss my friends from drunk driving school. They were so cool.'
jokes.setup: Homer worried about being made fun of by other bad dads
jokes.punchline: He fondly remembers his drunk driving school classmates
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy - Homer's nostalgia for fellow criminals shows his skewed priorities

jokes.text: Gerald's journal: 'When Derek saw me in the stands, his smile was worth a million dollars. Tuesday: Lost a million-dollar account because I left work early Monday.'
jokes.setup: Father sharing touching moment about son's happiness
jokes.punchline: The exact monetary cost of that emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff with perfect ironic connection between the metaphorical and literal million dollars

jokes.text: Homer describing strangling: 'I mean, it's not the only tool in my parenting toolbox, but... it's the sharpest.'
jokes.setup: Homer casually mentioning he strangles Bart
jokes.punchline: Describes strangling as the 'sharpest tool' in his parenting arsenal
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy that perfectly captures Homer's casual attitude toward violence and poor metaphor skills

jokes.text: Homer's father strangling young Homer: 'Look at these grades! They're a disgrace! No TV for a week! Why, you little...' followed by strangling
jokes.setup: Flashback to Homer's childhood
jokes.punchline: Shows the cycle of abuse - Homer learned strangling from his father
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing the generational pattern of the show's signature gag

jokes.text: Homer asking about butter cookies during serious therapy discussion: 'So, are those butter cookies for everybody, or?'
jokes.setup: Tense moment after Homer reveals he strangles his son
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the gravity and focuses on snacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - oblivious to social cues and focused on immediate pleasures

jokes.text: Dr. Zander turning chair around backwards: 'I'm going to turn my chair around backwards' to emphasize seriousness
jokes.setup: Dr. Zander dealing with serious situation
jokes.punchline: Announces his intention to use the cliché 'serious talk' chair position
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about therapy/counseling clichés - self-aware about the dramatic gesture

jokes.text: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar saying 'I was so disturbed I only scored 172 points. I had 86 assists.'
jokes.setup: Kareem explaining how upset he was about Homer's parenting
jokes.punchline: His 'poor' performance was still superhuman basketball stats
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo humor - absurd basketball stats used to measure emotional disturbance

jokes.text: Which is why you're the only Laker I could trust with this task.
jokes.setup: Kareem saying he has a good heart and doesn't want to hurt Homer
jokes.punchline: Implies other Lakers would be too violent/untrustworthy for therapy exercise
jokes.explanation: Mild sports humor about Lakers' reputation

jokes.text: Kareem: 'I don't want to hurt him, Doc.' Dr. Zander: 'You have a good heart, Kareem. Which is why you're the only Laker I could trust with this task.'
jokes.setup: Kareem showing reluctance to strangle Homer
jokes.punchline: Dr. Zander's specific Lakers-related reasoning for choosing Kareem
jokes.explanation: Good sports humor implying the other Lakers can't be trusted with therapeutic strangling duties

jokes.text: Gardener asking 'Uh, not a good time to water the plants?' during the strangling session, told 'No, it's fine'
jokes.setup: Intense therapy session with celebrity strangling patient
jokes.punchline: Mundane interruption treated as normal timing question
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist timing - mundane service interrupting bizarre therapy session treated casually

jokes.text: Dr. Zander asking Kareem 'what do you think about today's selfish players? Ball-hogging, tattooed...' during strangling session
jokes.setup: Intense therapeutic moment
jokes.punchline: Dr. Zander casually starting sports talk during the strangling
jokes.explanation: Good casual absurdity - inappropriate sports conversation during therapeutic violence

jokes.text: Homer: 'Dr. Zander, you made me see my life through Bart's neck'
jokes.setup: Homer expressing gratitude for therapy breakthrough
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing 'through his eyes' with the strangling context
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer malapropism - perfectly blends the therapy cliché with the specific method used

jokes.text: Homer admitting he lied about health insurance, Dr. Zander immediately responding 'And you're cured.'
jokes.setup: Dr. Zander planning years more therapy sessions
jokes.punchline: Instantly declares Homer cured when payment is revealed to be impossible
jokes.explanation: Perfect healthcare satire - therapy effectiveness directly tied to payment ability with instant turnaround

jokes.text: Homer telling Bart he can't quit drinking 'any more than I could quit being a man'
jokes.setup: Bart asking if Homer is cured of drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer treats drinking as an unchangeable fundamental trait like gender
jokes.explanation: Good character logic - Homer's twisted reasoning about what aspects of himself are changeable

jokes.text: Now it feels like morning.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably taking a morning drink
jokes.punchline: His day doesn't start until he's had alcohol
jokes.explanation: Dark character humor about Homer's dependency

jokes.text: Bart saying 'I'm in your head, man' when Homer struggles not to strangle him
jokes.setup: Homer fighting urge to strangle Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart recognizing his psychological power over his father
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal - Bart understanding his power to manipulate Homer's responses

jokes.text: Homer proudly saying 'Aw. My son's first stroke' when Bart has medical episode
jokes.setup: Bart appears to be having a stroke from stress
jokes.punchline: Homer treats serious medical emergency as a proud parenting milestone
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy - Homer's twisted perspective treating medical crisis as achievement

jokes.text: Homer's exasperated 'Oh, come on. Now you're a tree?' when Bart climbs up to escape
jokes.setup: Bart escaping Homer's reach by climbing a tree
jokes.punchline: Homer's frustrated personification of the tree as another obstacle
jokes.explanation: Good absurd logic - Homer blaming the tree itself rather than Bart's climbing ability

jokes.text: Bart telling Principal Skinner: 'He'd say, I'm a grown man who's scared of my own son.'
jokes.setup: Skinner asking what Homer would say about Bart's behavior
jokes.punchline: Bart accurately describes the power dynamic reversal
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Bart's accurate assessment of how the therapy backfired

jokes.text: I-I said, 'My keister is ready for a whooping, sir.'
jokes.setup: Homer mumbling something, Bart asking what he said
jokes.punchline: Homer's humiliating, submissive response
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor showing complete role reversal between father and son

jokes.text: Homer as magician: 'How many more, sir?' 'Fill the board, then wash my car. These are my keys.' 'I-I said, My keister is ready for a whooping, sir.'
jokes.setup: Homer desperately trying to get Bart to hit him in magic act
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly desperate and inappropriate attempts to provoke violence
jokes.explanation: Good escalating cringe - Homer's dignity completely eroding as he begs to be abused

jokes.text: It's 'A-B-C, one, two, three!' It's the easiest song in the world!
jokes.setup: Homer's nightmare about piano lessons as a child
jokes.punchline: His teacher's frustrated reaction to him failing at the most basic music education
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's childhood incompetence through exasperated teacher's perspective

jokes.text: Homer insulting kid: 'You think you're better than the Great Simpsini? Well, you're not. What are you gonna do, write a book about me? You couldn't get in the door of Random House if your name was Bennett Cerf III.'
jokes.setup: Homer as failed magician taking out frustrations on child
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific publishing industry reference as ultimate insult
jokes.explanation: Great obscure reference humor - Bennett Cerf publishing reference is perfectly weird for Homer's breakdown

jokes.text: Homer's nightmare insult: 'I don't see many more movie roles for a girl like you. Not unless George Lucas needs another Death Star.'
jokes.setup: Homer's nightmare about becoming a cruel person
jokes.punchline: Brutal physical appearance comparison to Death Star
jokes.explanation: Effective mean-spirited humor in dream context - cruel but well-constructed insult

jokes.text: Girl in dream crying 'No! Stop! No Star Wars parodies!'
jokes.setup: Homer making Death Star comparison
jokes.punchline: Character pleading specifically against Star Wars parodies rather than just insults
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor - character aware of being in a show that overuses Star Wars references

jokes.text: Caterer offering 'Mushroom cap?' 'Those are pieces of broken glass.' 'Well, I'll pass that on to the chef.'
jokes.setup: Homeless caterer still trying to maintain professional service
jokes.punchline: Casual response to serving broken glass as food
jokes.explanation: Good absurd commitment to professional service despite circumstances

jokes.text: Dr. Zander asking 'Are there franks in the beans?' about Homer's payment offer
jokes.setup: Dr. Zander reduced to accepting beans as payment
jokes.punchline: Still trying to negotiate food quality despite desperate circumstances
jokes.explanation: Good fall-from-grace humor showing professional trying to maintain standards

jokes.text: Marge showing Lisa a horse movie title 'so sad I can't even say it'
jokes.setup: Marge describing extremely sad horse movies
jokes.punchline: Title so emotionally devastating she can't speak it aloud
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of sadness concept, though visual title is unseen by audience

jokes.text: Moe's prank call: 'Text message for I.M.A. Wiener. As you all can see, I.M.A. Wiener.'
jokes.setup: Moe receiving what appears to be prank text
jokes.punchline: Classic Moe's Tavern prank call format adapted to texting
jokes.explanation: Good modernization of classic Simpsons running gag, updating prank calls to text era

jokes.text: Moe's texting mishaps: 'Oh, damn it, I typed an F and not a D.' 'Oh, crap, I just donated $20 to Haiti!'
jokes.setup: Moe trying to text angrily
jokes.punchline: Autocorrect and fat-finger mistakes leading to accidental charity
jokes.explanation: Good modern technology humor showing generational gap with digital devices

jokes.text: Oh, this is a tough neck! Oh, it's so strong! Like an oak.
jokes.setup: Dr. Zander trying to strangle Homer as demonstration
jokes.punchline: Professional admiration for Homer's strangulation-resistant neck
jokes.explanation: Absurd admiration for Homer's neck strength during attempted therapy violence

jokes.text: Dr. Zander: 'Sue me for what? My home in a hollowed-out tree?'
jokes.setup: Homer threatening to sue the homeless therapist
jokes.punchline: Dr. Zander pointing out he has no assets to take
jokes.explanation: Good reality check humor - practical response to empty legal threat

tmdb_overview: Marge persuades Homer to enroll in a fathering enrichment class; Bart becomes a school bully.
